response.justification: The candidate brings genuine high-throughput systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, and strong TypeScript across two decades of shipping software. However, Python — a must-have — is absent from the resume entirely, the deep distributed-systems work is over five years old and in DLang, and there is little evidence of staff-scope responsibilities like directing technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder coordination, or operational on-call ownership. Worth a conversation given the AI-native alignment, but with clear probing on Python depth and strategic scope.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/second) with async I/O, caching, and performance tuning
Current hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks — directly matches the AI-native environment
Strong TypeScript/Node.js track record plus broad polyglot range, testing discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development), and open-source ownership
concerns: No Python listed anywhere on the resume, despite strong Python being a must-have skill
Limited evidence of staff-level scope: no examples of directing technical strategy, managing stakeholders across functions, or operating an on-call rotation
Deepest distributed-systems experience dates to 2018–2020 and used DLang; recent roles are product-focused full-stack work, and there is an unexplained ~8-month gap in 2020
